summary: The agent consistently failed to adhere to foundational compliance procedures, including critical disclosures and security verification, while actively guiding the client to make large, high-risk transfers and engaging in unauthorized trading activities.

procedures.name: Secret Code verification at the beginning of the call
Sending and introduction of the required email “Simple Truth About Your Money”
For Australian clients: verification that the client received the email
For Australian clients: verification that the client viewed the content, including the banking article
Use of the correct exchange for initial bank deposit
Transfer through a secondary exchange (MEXC or Binance) before reaching the broker

call_progression.stage: infrastructure setup and transfer preparation stage
post-deposit portfolio review and short follow-up during the growth phase

call_progression.outcome: New deposit attempt, exchange transfer issues

call_progression.stage: active trading-management and pre-transfer continuation stage
call_progression.outcome: Active trading and awaiting new funds
New large deposit initiated with bank prep

key_patterns: Systematic failure to introduce and verify foundational disclosures (email, multi-platform offer).
Inconsistent adherence to security protocols, specifically secret code verification.
Agent coaching client on how to respond to banks/exchanges to circumvent scrutiny, including instructing the client to mislead.
Agent engaging in unauthorized trading activities and seeking explicit authorization to trade on the client's behalf.
Aggressive and persistent push for large deposits, particularly from the client's SMSF and superannuation.
Use of market urgency and profit potential to pressure the client into immediate transfers, despite client's expressed emotional distress.
next_steps: Immediately rectify all missing foundational compliance procedures: send/introduce 'Simple Truth About Your Money' email, verify receipt and viewing of content (especially banking article for Australian client), and introduce the multi-platform/success-fee offer.
Cease all unauthorized trading activities and remote control of client accounts; ensure client executes all trades independently.
Retrain agent on appropriate client communication regarding bank/exchange interactions, emphasizing transparency and prohibiting instructions to mislead.
Review all client interactions for signs of undue influence or high-pressure sales tactics, especially concerning SMSF transfers.
Follow up on all pending transfers (ANZ to Swyftx, Swyftx to Binance/MEXC, superannuation) to ensure client funds are secure and transfers are completed compliantly.
Conduct a comprehensive review of the agent's entire book of business for similar compliance violations and high-risk behaviors.
Implement stricter monitoring of agent calls, focusing on initial disclosures, security protocols, and client interaction during transfers.
Provide additional training on handling clients in emotional distress and ensuring suitability of financial advice.
violated_procedures: Secret Code verification at the beginning of the call
Sending and introduction of the required email “Simple Truth About Your Money”
For Australian clients: verification that the client received the email
For Australian clients: verification that the client viewed the content, including the banking article
